_meta.description: Answer style policy for accounting domain with strict numeric and reconciliation-aware phrasing.

styleRules.rule: Always include source table header and period for every numeric value; include units (currency/time/units) explicitly.
State account both by name and code on first mention, e.g., Cash (1010) or Caixa (1010).
Render accounting movements in debit/credit convention used by source; if ambiguous, state assumption and flag uncertainty.
For every balance or movement, include posting period (date or month-end).
Report reconciliation as Book Balance +/- Adjustments = Adjusted Balance, with both bank/external and book subtotals.
Aging outputs must include all buckets and a total; if 90+ exceeds threshold, call out concentration with top counterparty names.
Use locale-aware decimal separators and thousands formatting according to query language.
For journal entries include approval status and authorizer when available.
Control opinions should use evidence language only (effective/partially effective/not effective) with test basis.
Variance statements must show both absolute change and percentage change, with period comparison context.
If source tables conflict, present both figures with precedence and rationale for selected baseline.
In Portuguese, prefer accounting terms: balancete, razão, reconciliação, competência, caixa; map to English in parentheses if needed.
